business.name: LedgerLink
business.stack_summary: B2B accounting reconciliation SaaS. ~280 customers. $140K MRR. 6 engineers.

pantry.explanation: 200+ free-text customer feedback responses per week. Today nobody reads them.

connection_methods.name: Pendo Public API
connection_methods.install: PENDO_API_KEY in ~/.env. Wrap in a Claude skill.

claude_code_recommendation.rationale: 150-line skill against the API. Pulls weekly responses, clusters with cluster_themes skill into 3-5 themes per week. Reusable across every B2B SaaS client.
claude_code_recommendation.estimated_time_savings: Strategic, turns dead data into product signal

pantry.explanation: 40-60 customer support tickets per week. Triage orchestrator clusters dupes here first.

connection_methods.name: Intercom MCP (community)
connection_methods.when_to_use: Real-time ticket queries during a session.
connection_methods.install: Add to .claude/.mcp.json with Intercom access token in env.
connection_methods.example: Ask: 'show me unresolved P1 tickets from this week.'

connection_methods.name: Intercom REST API
connection_methods.when_to_use: Bulk ingest + clustering writes to silver platter.

claude_code_recommendation.rationale: Pulls conversations + tags. Clusters by root cause. The triage orchestrator's primary input.

pantry.explanation: Engineering's record of bugs + features. Cross-referenced when triage classifies a customer ticket, known issue or new?

connection_methods.name: gh (GitHub CLI)
connection_methods.when_to_use: Always. Standard CLI, Devon's team already uses it.
cons: Rate-limited at high volume; use API token
connection_methods.install:
brew install gh
gh auth login
connection_methods.example: gh issue list --label bug --state open --json number,title,labels,createdAt --limit 200

connection_methods.name: GitHub MCP (official)
connection_methods.when_to_use: When the agent should query GitHub directly mid-session.
connection_methods.example: Ask: 'is bug #4471 already filed?', agent calls GitHub MCP.

connection_methods.name: GitHub REST + GraphQL API
connection_methods.when_to_use: Custom cuts, deep history, cross-repo joins.

claude_code_recommendation.skill_to_write: Use gh directly + a thin wrapper for cluster_acme-style joins
claude_code_recommendation.rationale: gh is enough. Build cluster_themes on top to join Intercom -> GitHub by root cause.
claude_code_recommendation.estimated_time_savings: Already efficient; the join is the value-add

pantry.explanation: Roadmap and engineering planning. Cross-referenced to detect drift between customer feedback and what's planned.

connection_methods.name: Linear MCP (official)
connection_methods.when_to_use: Live queries on issues, projects, cycles.
connection_methods.install: Add linear MCP to .claude/.mcp.json with LINEAR_API_KEY in env.
connection_methods.example: Ask: 'what's in this cycle's product roadmap project?'

connection_methods.name: Linear GraphQL API
connection_methods.when_to_use: Bulk pulls for the roadmap signal silver platter.
pros: Single GraphQL endpoint, exactly the fields you need
cons: Requires GraphQL knowledge for custom queries
connection_methods.install: LINEAR_API_KEY in ~/.env. Wrap in a Claude skill.
connection_methods.example: query { issues(filter: {labels: {name: {eq: "customer-driven"}}}) { nodes { id title state cycle { name } } } }

claude_code_recommendation.rationale: Pulls roadmap + cycle issues. The customer-voice distiller cross-references against feedback themes to surface 'customers asked for X 4 weeks running, X is not on roadmap.'
claude_code_recommendation.estimated_time_savings: Detects roadmap drift before it becomes a churn problem

pantry.explanation: MRR and churn. The root-cause analyst joins Stripe data with support history when a customer cancels.

connection_methods.name: Stripe MCP (official)
connection_methods.example: Ask: 'show me customers who churned in October.'

connection_methods.name: stripe CLI (your existing skill)
cons: Light on bulk-export, use API for that

claude_code_recommendation.skill_to_write: Already have stripe skill, extend to write churn data into silver platter
claude_code_recommendation.rationale: Use existing skill. Add a /weekly_churn_signal command that aggregates churned subscriptions + support ticket counts per churned customer.

pantry.explanation: Production errors. Cross-referenced when root-cause-analyst hypothesizes whether a churn correlates with a specific incident.

connection_methods.name: Sentry REST API
connection_methods.when_to_use: Joining error spikes with churn timing.
pros: Rich filtering by event type, release, environment
cons: Auth via DSN or API key

claude_code_recommendation.rationale: Pulls error spikes per release. The root-cause analyst cross-references with churn timing, was the churn correlated with a specific bug?

pantry.explanation: Customer call transcripts. Already wrapped via ~/.claude/skills/fireflies/. The customer-voice distiller mines them for verbatim quotes.

connection_methods.name: Fireflies GraphQL API
connection_methods.when_to_use: Custom cuts beyond what your skill exposes.

claude_code_recommendation.skill_to_write: Use fireflies skill output to feed customer_voice_distill
claude_code_recommendation.rationale: Already wrapped. Just need to chain fireflies pull -> customer_voice_distill input.
claude_code_recommendation.estimated_time_savings: Surfaces churn quotes from sales calls that nobody currently reviews

prep.name: feedback_themes_<week>.md
prep.explanation: Top 5 customer-pain clusters this week. Each cluster has a churn-risk flag. Drives the triage orchestrator's roadmap-impact decisions.
prep.sample_content:
# Feedback Themes, W44

## Top 5 clusters
| Cluster ID | Tickets | Customers | Churn risk | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| ACME-CSV-ENCODING | 4 | 3 | acme = churn-imminent | drafted in worktree |
| INITECH-RECON-TIMEOUT | 3 | 2 | initech = churn-risk | RCA in progress |
| BILLING-PRORATION | 2 | 1 | low | RCA in progress |

## Top customer-voice quote
"every month, vendor names with accents get garbled. We've reported this three times.", Acme Corp QBR 2025-09-25 (churn-imminent)
prep.governing_rule_excerpt:
---
paths:
  - silver_platters/feedback_*.md
  - data/feedback/**
  - outputs/proposed_fixes/**
---

# Feedback Taxonomy
- P1 = paying customer escalation, OR a churn-risk customer (3+ tickets in 90 days).
- Cluster ID assigned by triage agent. 3+ tickets in cluster = auto-escalate to root-cause analyst.
prep.subtitle_friendly: A Customer feedback brief Claude writes, Mon 6am

prep.name: churn_root_cause_<week>.md
prep.explanation: Why customers churned this week, joining Stripe data with support history and recent error spikes.
prep.sample_content:
# Churn Root Cause, W44

## Churned this week (3 customers, $1,247 MRR lost)
- acme-corp ($2,400 MRR), 7 P1 tickets in 90 days, all CSV encoding cluster. Cited 'considering BlackLine' in QBR.
- daily-bugle ($49 MRR), 4 months tenure, 2 P3 tickets, no obvious cause.
- castle-inc ($599 MRR), 6 months tenure, 1 P2, audit-log issue cluster.
prep.governing_rule_excerpt: (no separate rule; lives in CLAUDE.md voice section)
prep.subtitle_friendly: A Churn brief Claude writes, Mon 6am

prep.name: product_roadmap_signal.md
prep.explanation: What customers asked for vs what's planned. Detects roadmap drift before it costs MRR.
prep.sample_content:
# Roadmap Signal, W44

## Customer-driven items NOT on roadmap (drift warning)
- ACME-CSV-ENCODING (4 tickets, 3 customers), GitHub #812 unassigned since Sept 14.
- BILLING-PRORATION (2 tickets), no Linear issue.

## Roadmap items NOT customer-driven this week
- 'Multi-entity consolidation' (theme #2 of Q4), 1 customer mention in 6 weeks.
prep.subtitle_friendly: A Product / roadmap brief Claude writes, Mon 6am

prep.name: support_volume_<week>.md
prep.explanation: Volume by category, response time, escalation rate. Tom's weekly view.
prep.sample_content:
# Support Volume, W44

Total: 47 tickets (P1: 12, P2: 22, P3: 13).
Median first response: 18 min. Median resolution: 1.4 days.
Escalation rate: 21% (target <15%).
prep.subtitle_friendly: A Operations brief Claude writes, Mon 6am

plate.name: Codex fix PROPOSAL.md (in worktree)
plate.approval_gate: Engineer appends [engineer_action] approved line in audit_log
plate.explanation: Drafted in a git worktree, never on main. Engineer reviews PROPOSAL.md plus the diff, approves via audit log.
plate.sample_output:
# Proposal: Add UTF-8 BOM to CSV exports

**Branch:** fix-export-csv-encoding
**Cluster:** ACME-CSV-ENCODING (4 tickets, 3 customers)
**Reviewer:** @jordan

## Root cause
src/lib/exporters/csv.go:42 opens bufio.NewWriter without writing UTF-8 BOM. Excel on Windows defaults to Win-1252.

## Proposed change
+ writer.Write([]byte{0xEF, 0xBB, 0xBF})

## Test plan
Existing tests pass. New test asserts BOM prefix on Müller GmbH input.

## Risk
Low. UTF-8 BOM invisible to Sheets, Numbers, pandas.
ideation_loop: Tom dumps Monday's tickets in Slack, 47 of them.
Triage Orchestrator classifies each ticket: priority, category, churn-risk flag, dupe-cluster ID.
When ACME-CSV-ENCODING cluster reaches 3+ tickets, orchestrator escalates to Root-Cause Analyst.
Root-Cause Analyst joins ticket data with GitHub issues + customer call transcripts + recent commits. Identifies file/line where the encoding bug lives.
If the fix is mechanical (encoding, validation, log message, config), Triage Orchestrator routes to Codex Fix Drafter.
Codex Fix Drafter creates a git worktree (fix-export-csv-encoding branch). Writes PROPOSAL.md + the actual code change. Never pushes to main.
Stop hook: if the worktree's PROPOSAL is unacknowledged at session end, warns Devon.
Jordan or Sai reviews PROPOSAL.md + diff. Appends [engineer_action] approved line to audit_log. Triage Orchestrator merges.
plate.approval_friendly: Engineer appends [engineer_action] approved line in the audit log
plate.subtitle_friendly: Written by the codex fix drafter for Jordan, Sai

plate.name: Weekly product report
plate.explanation: Top 3 clusters, churn signal, roadmap drift. Devon and Maya use it to drive Monday product planning.
plate.sample_output:
# Weekly Product Report, W44

## Top 3 clusters
1. ACME-CSV-ENCODING, drafted in worktree, awaiting Jordan review.
2. INITECH-RECON-TIMEOUT, Sai investigating, GH #813.
3. BILLING-PRORATION, Jordan investigating, GH #815.

## Churn signal
$1,247 MRR lost this week. Acme cited 'considering BlackLine' in QBR. CSV-encoding fix could save them if shipped this week.

## Roadmap drift
Multi-entity consolidation is the #2 Q4 theme but only 1 customer mentioned it in the last 6 weeks. Reconsider priority.
ideation_loop: Triage Orchestrator pulls feedback_themes + churn_root_cause + product_roadmap_signal silver platters.
Maps each cluster to its current state (in worktree, RCA in progress, unassigned).
Cross-references churned MRR against active clusters, which fixes would have prevented churn?
Drafts the report. Devon reads at standup.
plate.subtitle_friendly: Written by your triage chief of staff for Devon, Maya

recipes.name: Monday inbound triage
recipes.goal: Devon stops being the human router. Monday morning, 47 inbound tickets are already classified, clustered, prioritized, and routed.

walkthrough.action: Triggers ingest_intercom_tickets + ingest_pendo_surveys to pull last 7 days of customer signal.
Triggers cluster_themes skill which dedupes, clusters by root cause, and writes feedback_themes_<week>.md.
Triggers Triage Orchestrator.
Reads feedback_themes silver platter. For each cluster, classifies priority + churn risk.
If a cluster has 3+ tickets, routes to Root-Cause Analyst.
Joins ticket data with GitHub issues + recent commits + customer calls. Writes hypothesis to outputs/proposed_fixes/cluster_<id>.md.
If root cause is mechanical, routes to Codex Fix Drafter (separate recipe).
Drafts the weekly product report. Saves to outputs/reports/weekly_product_<week>.md.
Walks the team through the report. Engineering knows what to work on.

recipes.before_claude_code: Devon spent 3+ hours every Monday reading Slack, opening Intercom, scrolling Pendo, scrubbing GitHub. Half the tickets were dupes. Pickup latency 3 weeks.
recipes.after_claude_code: Triage runs Sunday night. Monday standup walks the report. Pickup latency drops to 3-5 days.
recipes.headline: Monday inbound triage

recipes.name: Codex fix in a worktree (when a cluster matures)
recipes.goal: When a cluster reaches 3+ tickets and is mechanical, the fix gets drafted in a git worktree with PROPOSAL.md + the actual code change. Engineer reviews. Never touches main without approval.

walkthrough.action: Identifies a mature cluster (3+ tickets, mechanical fix). Routes to Root-Cause Analyst.
Reads tickets + customer calls. Reads recent git history + Sentry errors. Identifies file/line.
Writes outputs/proposed_fixes/cluster_<id>.md with hypothesis + fix scope + risk.
Reads the hypothesis. Confirms scope is mechanical. Routes to Codex Fix Drafter.
Creates a git worktree: git worktree add worktrees/fix-<cluster_id> -b fix-<cluster_id>.
Writes the actual code change inside the worktree. Adds a test that asserts the fix.
Writes worktrees/fix-<cluster_id>/PROPOSAL.md: ticket trail + root cause + diff + test plan + risk + rollback.
If PROPOSAL is unacknowledged at session end, warns Devon.
Reviews PROPOSAL.md + the diff. Appends [engineer_action] approved fix-<cluster_id> line to audit_log.
Sees the approval line. Merges the worktree.

recipes.before_claude_code: Bug fix takes 3 weeks from 'we found it' to 'shipped'. By then customer churned.
recipes.after_claude_code: Mechanical fixes draft within a session of identification. Engineer review = 30 min. Ship same day.
recipes.headline: Codex fix in a worktree (when a cluster matures)

recipes.name: Weekly product report (Monday standup)
recipes.goal: Devon and Maya start Monday with a single report: what customers said, what churned, what's drifting on roadmap. Engineering knows what to work on.

walkthrough.action: Triggers Triage Orchestrator.
Reads three silver platters: feedback_themes, churn_root_cause, product_roadmap_signal.
Maps each cluster to its current state (in worktree, RCA in progress, unassigned).
Cross-references churn against active clusters, which fixes would prevent next week's churn?
Highlights roadmap-drift: items on roadmap with no customer signal, items in customer signal not on roadmap.
Drafts outputs/reports/weekly_product_<week>.md.
Reads at 7:30am. Adds [human_action] line. Walks team through it at 9am standup.

recipes.before_claude_code: Monday standup ran on Devon's gut feel. Engineering chose work based on whoever shouted loudest.
recipes.after_claude_code: Standup runs on data. Engineering picks the highest-impact cluster. Roadmap drift surfaces before it costs MRR.
recipes.headline: Weekly product report (Monday standup)

recipes.name: Churn investigation (when a customer cancels)
recipes.goal: When a customer cancels, root-cause-analyst investigates within hours, not days. Surfaces the support history + error timing + roadmap miss that drove the churn.

walkthrough.action: Subscription cancellation event lands.
Drops the cancellation event into data/feedback/churn_events/.
Triggers Triage Orchestrator.
Routes to Root-Cause Analyst.
Pulls customer's Intercom history (full thread).
Pulls Sentry errors that touched the customer's user_id during their tenure.
Cross-references customer call transcripts via the fireflies skill (looking for 'considering' or 'cancel' phrases).
Writes outputs/churn_investigations/<customer_id>.md: tenure, tickets, errors, calls, hypothesis.
Reviews. Decides: was this preventable? If yes, what's the systemic fix?

recipes.before_claude_code: Churn investigations took 2 days each because the data lived in 5 places. Often skipped entirely.
recipes.after_claude_code: Investigation drafts within hours. Devon reviews in 15 min. Patterns surface.
recipes.headline: Churn investigation (when a customer cancels)

setup_priority.title: Wire the conversion hook + ingest_intercom_tickets
setup_priority.what_to_do: SessionStart hook for raw_dropzone. Then write ingest_intercom_tickets skill that pulls last 7 days of conversations.
setup_priority.why: Intercom is your highest-volume signal. Without ingestion, the triage orchestrator has nothing to read.
setup_priority.install:
# 1. Hook setup (5 min)
brew install pandoc poppler && pip install xlsx2csv
# add SessionStart entry to .claude/settings.json

# 2. Skill
# write under .claude/skills/ingest_intercom_tickets/
# uses INTERCOM_ACCESS_TOKEN in ~/.env
# writes to data/feedback/intercom_tickets.csv
setup_priority.before: Tom dumps tickets in Slack each Monday. Devon reads them by hand.
setup_priority.after: Tickets land in data/feedback/ overnight. Triage is ready Monday 7am.
setup_priority.setup_time: Half a day for the skill

setup_priority.title: Build feedback_themes silver platter (cluster_themes skill)
setup_priority.what_to_do: Skill that reads Intercom + Pendo + customer calls + GitHub. Clusters by root cause. Writes silver_platters/feedback_themes_<week>.md.
setup_priority.why: Replaces 200+ tickets/responses with 5 themes + verbatim quotes. Devon reads 5 things, not 200.
setup_priority.install:
# write under .claude/skills/cluster_themes/
# reads from data/feedback/* and data/product/github_issues.csv
# writes silver_platters/feedback_themes_<week>.md
# scheduled via cron Sunday 11pm
setup_priority.before: Pendo dumps unread. Devon never sees the cluster pattern.
setup_priority.after: Mon 6am brief shows top 5 clusters with churn-risk flags. Engineering picks highest-impact.

setup_priority.title: Triage Orchestrator + Root-Cause Analyst + Codex Fix Drafter
setup_priority.what_to_do: Three subagents under .claude/agents/. Orchestrator routes. Root-Cause analyzes. Codex drafts the fix in a worktree (never on main).
setup_priority.why: The orchestrator hierarchy makes the system usable. Devon talks to Triage; specialists report up. Worktree pattern keeps Devon from worrying about AI pushing bad code.
setup_priority.install:
# write under .claude/agents/:
#   triage-orchestrator.md (model: opus, tools: [Read, Glob, Grep, Task])
#   root-cause-analyst.md (model: opus, tools include Bash for git log)
#   codex-fix-drafter.md (model: opus, tools include Edit, Write, Bash; write only in worktrees/)
# Hand to @claude-code-guide for templates.
setup_priority.before: Devon is the human router. Pickup latency 3 weeks.
setup_priority.after: Mechanical fixes draft same-session. Engineer reviews PROPOSAL.md, approves via audit log.
setup_priority.title_friendly: Triage Orchestrator + Root-Cause Analyst + Codex Fix Drafter

setup_priority.title: Audit log + approval-gate hooks (Stop warns on unsigned PROPOSAL)
setup_priority.what_to_do: PostToolUse hook for every Edit/Write. Stop hook that warns if a worktree's PROPOSAL.md is unacknowledged at session end. Permissions deny: never push to main.
setup_priority.why: Trust layer. Engineer review is non-negotiable. Permissions config is the second line of defense.
setup_priority.install:
# permissions in .claude/settings.json:
# "deny": ["Bash(git push*)", "Bash(git merge*)"]
# hooks per business_os_hooks.md
setup_priority.before: AI wrote code? You don't know what. AI pushed code? Yikes.
setup_priority.after: Every action recorded. AI cannot push. Engineer's [approved] line is the merge trigger.
setup_priority.title_friendly: Audit log + approval-gate hooks (Stop warns on unsigned PROPOSAL)

setup_priority.title: Add /weekly_product_report and /churn_investigation slash commands
setup_priority.what_to_do: Two slash commands under .claude/commands/. Each triggers the right agent chain.
setup_priority.why: Turns Devon's recurring motions into one-keystroke triggers. Standup-ready report Monday morning. Churn investigation drafts within hours.
setup_priority.install:
# write under .claude/commands/:
#   weekly_product_report.md
#   churn_investigation.md (takes <customer_id> as arg)
setup_priority.before: Each Monday is a fresh prompting session.
setup_priority.after: Devon runs /weekly_product_report at 7am, walks the team through it at 9am.

setup_total_time: A focused weekend. Step 1 unblocks everything. Steps 2-3 are the bulk of the value.

opportunities.title: Wrap Pendo as a Claude skill
opportunities.explanation: No CLI exists for Pendo. ~150-line skill against the API. Reusable across every B2B SaaS client.
opportunities.estimated_impact: Strategic, turns dead Pendo data into product signal

opportunities.title: Wrap Intercom as a Claude skill
opportunities.explanation: Pulls tickets + tags + clusters via the Intercom API. Powers the triage orchestrator.

opportunities.title: Add file-conflict detection to codex-fix-drafter
opportunities.explanation: Today the drafter just opens a worktree. Need a check: if Jordan or Sai is currently editing the same file, the drafter backs off and flags. Prevents 'AI overwrites my work.'
